channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 4 First Trailer Highlights Amy Rose
item.content:encoded:
Sonic the Hedgehog 4 is set to release in 2027, and the first teaser trailer has been unveiled.

The Sonic the Hedgehog movie series has revitalized the iconic Sega video game franchise. The fourth film has already been confirmed to feature Amy Rose, voiced by Kristen Bell.

The new trailer confirms the return of the main cast, including Jim Carrey as Dr. Eggman, Idris Elba as Knuckles the Echidna, and Keanu Reeves as Shadow the Hedgehog.

The teaser starts with Amy’s hammer, then shifts to the Chaos Emeralds and features the main characters. The logo also includes the number four in pink, indicating that Amy will be a central figure in the movie.

First teaser for ‘SONIC 4’. In theaters on March 19, 2027.

Full Cast of the Upcoming Sonic the Hedgehog Movie

In addition to Amy Rose, Sir Ben Kingsley has been cast in a significant role in “Sonic the Hedgehog 4,” though his role wasn’t revealed in the trailer.

The third Sonic film focused on Sonic Adventure 2 and introduced Shadow, ending with a teaser featuring Amy Rose. Sega first introduced Amy in Sonic CD (1993), where Metal Sonic was the antagonist.

Variety has also revealed more of the movie’s cast. This movie will feature a Parks and Recreation reunion for Ben Schwartz (voice of Sonic) and Nick Offerman. Offerman has been confirmed to join the cast. Other cast members include Tika Sumpter, Matt Berry, Colleen O’Shaughnessey, Lee Majdoub, and Richard Ayoade.

Recently, Sega announced plans for an animated series based on classic games like Streets of Rage and OutRun. More details can be found at this link.

What are you hoping to see for the next Sonic the Hedgehog movie? Are there any characters that you’re hoping get added to the movie? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 1 Quests & How To Complete Them (March 19)
item.content:encoded:
Your favorite battle royale shooter is back in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2. With new storylines, skins, rewards, and all the usual antics that come with new seasonal content, let’s focus on the big thing for now—Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 1 challenges.

The new season was barely a few hours old when the community was asking questions left, right, and center: Should you choose team Ice King or Team Foundation in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2? Or what is the Rivalry Screen?

All good queries, but what is known is the first lot of weekly challenges.

Every Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 1 Quest & Reward

The first week of Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 comes with nine new quests to start, and they range from nailing some accurate headshots on enemy players to finding and using Skyline Deployers.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Showdown Challenges & Rewards

Check out every Week 1 challenge in our handy table below.

When Do Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 2 Quests Release?

Providing Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 carries on the same trend as previous seasons, the Week 2 challenges should drop one week later—giving you a March 26 window for the next challenges.

As usual, the weekly challenge grind is to keep you coming back for more! If Epic gave you all the challenges at once, it would be overwhelming, let’s face it.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Weapons—All New & Unvaulted Weapons

So, you’ve got seven days to clean up every Week 1 challenge, and one week later, it’ll be time for the Week 2 challenges in Chapter 7 Season 2.

Leave a comment in the Insider Gaming Discord telling the team (or fellow Fortnite fans) what you think of the first challenges. Do they look tough? Do you have your own tips to complete them and make the objectives easier?

Make sure you check out the news that Fortnite creators can soon make Star Wars Battlefront-Style Islands, if you haven’t already!

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: The Last Caretaker Update Finally Adds Human Contact
item.content:encoded:
The Last Caretaker is about to get another major update, this one dubbed ‘Curiosity Wakes’. It’s the game’s third major update since hitting early access in November 2025. This significant patch finally introduces humanity to the isolated game world, settling a fan theory once and for all.

This promises to be a hugely impactful change, but there’s plenty more lined up for Curiosity Wakes beyond that. Read on to learn what you need to know.

Curiosity Wakes Brings Human Interaction

For those not in the know, The Last Caretaker is a beautiful but haunting game that puts players in control of just that, a last Caretaker. This is a robot alone at the end of the world, bound on a mission to launch human embryos into space, to the final bastions of civilization residing in the stars.

It’s an open-world sandbox title set in a world that has been ravaged by rising tides, rendering it traversable exclusively by boat. The world is now populated by mysterious, aggressive creatures, a few remnants of humanity’s great buildings, and resources that you must use to build, craft, and ultimately, survive.

Channel37, the game’s developer, just unveiled Curiosity Wakes, the third major update to hit The Last Caretaker since it dropped late last year.

Here’s the one-liner:

Curiosity Wakes marks a pivotal shift in the game’s direction by shattering the silence of the heavens and unlocking the player’s ability to contact humanity’s distant orbital haven for the very first time.

Until now, fans of The Last Caretaker had theorized that nobody was left up there in the vast expanse, with humanity having long since died out just because of the natural passing of time. Effectively, players would be launching embryos to space, but nobody would be up there to receive them.

In Curiosity Wakes, we learn that’s not the case.

Players will take on new creatures (including Laser Sharks and Arch Angels), build more constructs than ever before, and customize their Caretaker with reckless abandon. Also, Channel37 is adding new locations, quests, and upgrades to the game, expanding it quite nicely.

Curiosity Wakes is available now, exclusively on PC.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve played The Last Caretaker.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the Hitman TV show is dead

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: What is The Rivalry Screen in Fortnite? Chapter 7 Season 2 Rivalries, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 has finally launched, having previously been delayed for two weeks, and the Rivalry Screen is a new feature this season.

Epic Games has provided a slew of new updates as part of the seasonal transition, alongside the standard map changes and release of a new Battle Pass, and the Rivalry Screen is one of the most eye-catching.

If you want all the details on the Fortnite Rivalry Screen, Insider Gaming has you covered.

What is The Rivalry Screen in Fortnite?

The Rivalry Screen in Fortnite is new to Chapter 7 Season 2 and provides players with a particular target to hunt down in a match.

In the Rivalry Screen, you can select a player in the match as a Rival. Additionally, in the first few days of the season, players will automatically have a Rival selected soon after the match begins.

  • More Insider Gaming: All New & Unvaulted Weapons in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2

Eliminations and damage are tracked across matches to determine your rivals, and Rivalry Screens prioritize players you have the most recent history with. Additionally, a Rematch button will be added later in the season, allowing you to search for a new match with other players who have selected the same option.

The more you use the Rivalry Screen, the more often you will appear as a Rival for another player. If you skip the screens in consecutive matches, you will be targeted less often.

How to Get & Use Rival Credits in Chapter 7 Season 2

Rival Credits are earned by eliminating your Rival in matches and are spent at Rivalry Gear machines to earn a variety of rewards.

There are 20 Rivalry Ranks to progress through, with higher ranks unlocking better items at Rivalry Gear machines. Available items include infinite Slap, Exotic weapons, and once-a-day XP bundles.

Rival Credits do not reset at the end of a game, and instead persist between matches. As such, you can save up your Rival Credits to splurge in a particular game of your choosing.

How to Get a New Rival in Chapter 7 Season 2

Earning a new Rival in a Fortnite match can be done in several different ways, though there are some restrictions.

If you are rebooted in a match, the player who eliminated you automatically becomes your rival, and taking them out provides double the amount of Rival Credits.

  • More Insider Gaming: Should You Choose Team Ice King or Team Foundation?

In a case where your Rival is eliminated by another player, the eliminated player drops a Rivalry Contract. If you reach it first, the elimination will be attributed to you.

If another player picks up the Rivalry Contract before you, the rivalry will continue with that player as your new target.

Do you like the new Rivals system in Fortnite? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, check out all the Showdown Challenges and rewards, and take a look at how to get the Launch Week Bonus bundle.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crystal Dynamics Hit With Another Round of Layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Crystal Dynamics has announced it is holding another round of layoffs, this time letting go of 20 employees.

The upcoming titles from the developer, Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis and Tomb Raider: Catalyst, are currently in full development. However, they appear to be continually laying off employees. A little over four months ago, the company announced it would eliminate 30 positions, and now 20 more will also be dismissed.

Crystal Dynamics Holds Fourth Round of Layoffs Within a Year

A new LinkedIn post from the official Crystal Dynamics account states that the company is “reducing its workforce by 20 talented team members.” The dismissed employees were split between development personnel and some central operations roles. The studio has stated that, as it moves into new phases of development, it looks at “team structures to ensure they align with our long-term studio goals” and deems these departures “necessary”.

The Tomb Raider developer has announced several rounds of layoffs within the past year. The first one was in March 2025, where they made the “difficult decision” to let go of 17 people from the company. The next one arrived in August 2025, when an undisclosed number of employees were let go. Finally, the third one arrived in November 2025, where they parted ways with just under 30 team members across various departments.

In other news, Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis’ difficulty will be adjusted for “modern player tastes”. Additionally, Catalyst developers have revealed that players don’t need to do any homework before jumping in. What are your thoughts on Crystal Dynamics announcing another round of layoffs? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite players getting to create Star Wars islands from May 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Showdown Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
A slew of new content is available in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, including new Showdown challenges to earn in-game rewards, and a global leaderboard with physical prizes available.

Fortnite‘s Showdown challenges are geared around the theme of Chapter 7 Season 2, which sees The Foundation, a returning member of The Seven, face off against the Ice King.

For all the details on the Showdown Weekly Challenges, the available rewards, and the physical rewards you can earn, Insider Gaming has you covered.

How to Sign Up For Fortnite Showdown Rewards

While some in-game rewards are earned through progression, others require signing up via the official Fortnite Showdown website to earn additional rewards.

After selecting Team Ice King or Team Foundation, head to the Fortnite Showdown website and accept the terms and conditions to sign up for additional rewards.

Rewards range from additional in-game rewards, earned by completing special weekly challenges, to physical rewards including Nvidia Graphics Cards and merchandise.

All Showdown Rewards & Challenges, Listed

Various in-game rewards are available in Fortnite Showdown during Chapter 7 Season 2, distributed after you complete specific challenges.

The event period is set to last for the opening four weeks of the season, with new challenges dropping, though it’s not clear how long after the drop of the final weekly challenge they will remain available.

  • More Insider Gaming: Should You Choose Team Foundation or Team Ice King?

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 is due to end on June 5, but the rewards may disappear before that date rolls around.

The full list of Showdown Challenges and rewards is shown in the table below. This will be updated weekly when new challenges are revealed.

All Showdown Physical Rewards & How to Earn

The top players in the world will receive physical rewards during Fortnite’s Showdown event, though there are some restrictions.

The Global Leaderboard Challenge kicks off on March 19, alongside the new season launch, and will remain available until April 16 at 23:59 ET.

  • More Insider Gaming: All New & Unvaulted Weapons in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2

To be eligible for rewards, players must have a valid Epic Games account and have selected Team Ice King or Team Foundation to participate in the challenge.

All of the available physical rewards for the Fortnite Showdown event and the requirements to earn them are shown in the table below.

Which reward have you got your eyes on in the Fortnite Showdown event? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, find out how to get the Launch Week Bonus, and check out details of the Star Wars items available in Creative.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
